title: Greeting Each Argument
prompt_md:
## Goal

A real CLI greets every name passed *after* the program name on the command line. The thing the lesson is actually about is the shape of `main(int argc, char* argv[])`: `argc` counts **every** token including the program name, and the user's arguments live at `argv[1]`, `argv[2]`, ... -- never `argv[0]`.

Because the sandbox cannot pass real `argv`, the command line arrives on stdin and is loaded into an `argc`/`argv` pair for you. All the reading, looping, and output formatting are scaffolded. Your job is the two argument-handling decisions: (1) use `argc` to decide whether the user supplied any names at all, and (2) start iterating at the index where real arguments begin so you skip the program name.

## Input

- Line 1: an integer `argc` (the full argument count, **including** the program name, so it is always >= 1).
- The next `argc` lines: one argument string each. The first is the program name (`argv[0]`); the rest are names to greet (names may contain spaces).

## Output

- For each user argument, in order: `Hello, <name>!`
- If there are no user arguments: print exactly `No names provided.`

title: Numeric Arguments
prompt_md:
## Goal

Command line arguments always arrive as **text**, even when they represent numbers, so a CLI must convert each one before doing math. That conversion -- turning a textual `argv` token into a usable `int` with `std::stoi` -- is exactly what this lesson is about, and it is the only logic you write here.

Because the sandbox cannot pass real `argv`, the command line arrives on stdin and is loaded into an `argc`/`argv` pair for you, with `argv[0]` being the program name. The reading and the output are scaffolded, along with the driving loop. Inside the loop you must convert the current textual argument to an integer and fold it into the running sum and max -- and choose the loop's starting index so you skip the program name. You are guaranteed at least one numeric argument.

## Input

- Line 1: an integer `argc` (2 <= argc <= 101), the full token count including the program name.
- The next `argc` lines: one token each. The first is the program name; the rest are integers written **as text** (values may be negative).

## Output

Exactly two lines:
```
Sum: <total>
Max: <largest>
```
